company.title: Company profile
company.subtitle: This description is given to the AI model so it can explain why each news signal matters for your business and draft relevant outreach snippets.
company.offeringPlaceholder: Describe what your company sells, who it's for, and the problems it solves...
company.mainLanguageHint: The standard language for the interface and AI-generated signal summaries. Users can still override it for themselves in their own profile.
company.maxArticlesPerCompany: Max articles per company per fetch (0 = no limit)
company.maxArticlesPerCompanyHint: Caps each company to its newest N articles per fetch run before they're summarized — protects against a single busy company (or a broad keyword list) using up the whole run's AI budget.

targets.title: My companies
targets.subtitle: Add the companies you want news signals for. Keywords/aliases help match more articles.
targets.keywordsPlaceholder: Type a keyword and press Enter
targets.keywordsHint: Prefer specific names/domains over generic single words or abbreviations — an overly broad keyword can pull in unrelated articles that don't actually mention this company.
targets.backfillTitle: Pull historical coverage for this company from NewsData.io's archive
targets.confirmDeleteAdmin: This permanently deletes "{{name}}" and all its signals for every user. Continue?
targets.confirmUnfollowOnly: You're the only follower — unfollowing "{{name}}" removes it (and its signals) for everyone. Continue?
targets.confirmUnfollowShared: Unfollow "{{name}}"? Other users tracking it keep seeing it.

sources.title: News sources
sources.subtitle: Which providers ingestion pulls from, each with its own enforced rate limit — a source stops being called for the rest of a run once its limit is reached, rather than only logging the overage afterward. Usage against each limit is shown inline below it.

googleNewsRss.hint: Free, keyless public feed. Google publishes no official quota for it, so the limit below is a self-imposed politeness ceiling, not a real plan tier.

newsdata.hint: Paid API — leans on full-article-content grounding, native duplicate removal, native sentiment/tags, and a one-time historical backfill when a company is added.
newsdata.fetchFullContent: Fetch full article content when the plan includes it (better-grounded summaries)
newsdata.backfillHint: When set, newly-created companies automatically get a one-time historical pull covering this many days via NewsData.io's archive endpoint.
newsdata.apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter a new key to set or rotate it

sources.noApiKeyConfigured: No API key configured — set one below or via the {{envVar}} environment variable.
sources.usingEnvKey: Using a key from the server's environment variable{{suffix}}.
sources.clearNewsdataKeyToast: In-app NewsData.io API key override cleared.

ai.title: AI configuration
ai.subtitle: Controls how Mistral is used to summarize articles — which models are called, how aggressively duplicate coverage is filtered, and which API key is used.
ai.preFilterLabel: Pre-filter articles with the triage model before full summarization (recommended — cuts cost by skipping low-value articles before the expensive call)
ai.dedupeHint: Lower the threshold to dedupe more aggressively (more distinct stories may get merged); raise it to only merge near-identical coverage.
ai.apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter a new key to set or rotate it

usage.title: AI usage
usage.subtitle: Mistral token usage over the last {{days}} days, broken down by pipeline stage so you can see the cost impact of duplicate-skipping and triage filtering.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Promote or demote users. Admins manage workspace settings and can assign companies to any user.
users.lastAdminHint: You may be the last remaining admin
users.assignTitle: Assign a company to a user
users.assignSubtitle: Select an existing catalog company by typing its exact name, or a new name to create it. The user sees it in their dashboard immediately — no action needed from them.
users.chooseUserFirst: Choose a user to assign this company to.

logs.title: Ingestion logs
logs.subtitle: History of every "Fetch new signals" run, manual or scheduled, including any errors encountered while pulling from a news source or summarizing an article.
